title: Igroup Presence Questionnaire (IPQ)
description: JSON schema for the IPQ — 14-item Likert self-report scale measuring sense of presence in virtual environments. Based on Schubert, Friedmann & Regenbrecht (2001).
citation: Schubert, T., Friedmann, F., & Regenbrecht, H. (2001). The experience of presence: Factor analytic insights. Presence, 10(3), 266-281.

scale.note: Some items use 0-6 in digital implementations; always check anchor labelling in your tool.

SP.name: Spatial Presence
SP.description: The sense of being physically located inside the virtual environment

INV.name: Involvement
INV.description: Attention devoted to the VE and involvement with it

REAL.name: Experienced Realism
REAL.description: Subjective experience of realism in the VE

G.name: General Presence
G.description: Single-item overall presence rating (not in factor solution but often reported)

G1.text: Somehow I felt that the virtual world surrounded me.

SP1.text: I had a sense of acting in the virtual space.

SP2.text: I felt present in the virtual space.

SP3.text: I did not feel present in the virtual space.

SP4.text: I had a sense of being there.

SP5.text: The virtual space felt real to me.

INV2.text: I was not aware of my real environment.

INV3.text: I paid attention to the virtual environment.

INV4.text: My attention was drawn to the virtual environment.

REAL1.text: The virtual world seemed real to me.

REAL3.text: The objects in the virtual space seemed real.

REAL4.text: The virtual environment did not seem realistic.

composite_scoring.reverse_coding: Reversed items must be recoded before subscale averaging: recoded = max_anchor - raw + min_anchor

psychometric_properties.internal_consistency: Cronbach alpha SP=0.80, INV=0.71, REAL=0.75 (original validation)
